text: Testing plays a crucial role in software development. One common approach is unit tests, which check individual components of code to ensure they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of the system interact properly together. A popular method is test-driven development, or TDD, where developers write a test before writing the actual code. This practice helps clarify requirements and prevents bugs early on. Ultimately, thorough testing builds confidence, allowing developers to make changes or add features without fear of breaking existing functionality.
